skill.name: lark-wiki
skill.description: 飞书知识库：管理知识空间和文档节点。创建和查询知识空间、管理节点层级结构、在知识库中组织文档和快捷方式。当用户需要在知识库中查找或创建文档、浏览知识空间结构、移动或复制节点时使用。
skill.summary: Manage Lark (Feishu) knowledge bases, document nodes, and workspace structure
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' patterns are markdown code blocks containing documentation examples, not executable code. 'Path traversal' detection is a relative path reference in markdown documentation. No actual security risks present in this documentation-only skill file.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 8 instances of 'external_commands' and 2 instances of 'filesystem' patterns. These are all markdown documentation elements: code blocks showing CLI usage examples (lines 17-20, 22, 26, 32), inline code for file paths (line 13), and relative path references in documentation text. No executable code is present.

content.user_title: Manage Lark Knowledge Base with CLI
content.value_statement: Organize and navigate Lark (Feishu) knowledge bases through CLI commands. Query spaces, manage document nodes, and structure workspace hierarchies efficiently.
actual_capabilities: Query Lark knowledge space nodes and retrieve node information
View API schema structures for wiki resources before calling APIs
Execute wiki API methods through lark-cli command interface
Access documentation for required permissions and scopes
Reference shared authentication setup from lark-shared module
limitations: Requires lark-cli binary to be installed on the system
Depends on external lark-shared/SKILL.md for authentication setup
Only supports spaces.get_node method in current implementation
Requires proper API permissions and scopes configured separately

use_cases.title: Query Knowledge Space Structure
use_cases.description: Developers working with Lark/Feishu need to retrieve information about knowledge space nodes to understand document hierarchy and permissions.

use_cases.title: Document Organization Automation
use_cases.description: Teams automating documentation workflows can query wiki node information to programmatically navigate and organize knowledge bases.

use_cases.title: API Schema Exploration
use_cases.description: Users exploring Lark wiki APIs can use schema commands to understand parameter structures before making API calls.

prompt_templates.title: Get Wiki Node Information
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the node information for the Lark wiki space with node ID {node_id}
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to retrieve details about a specific wiki node including its type and parent node

prompt_templates.title: Check API Schema Before Call
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the schema for wiki.spaces.get_node method before I call the API
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to understand the required parameters and data structure before making an API call

prompt_templates.title: List Available Wiki Methods
prompt_templates.prompt: What wiki API methods are available and what permissions do they need?
prompt_templates.scenario: User exploring the capabilities of the lark-wiki skill and understanding required scopes

prompt_templates.title: Verify Authentication Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to call the wiki API. Help me check the authentication requirements from lark-shared
prompt_templates.scenario: User setting up Lark CLI for the first time needs to understand authentication prerequisites

output_examples.input: Get wiki node information for node_id 123456
output_examples.output: Retrieved node information: Node type is wiki.space, parent node is none, requires wiki:node:read permission scope

output_examples.input: Show me the schema for wiki.spaces.get_node
output_examples.output: API schema: Requires --data parameter with node_id field. Returns object containing node_type, parent_node_id, and creator details

best_practices: Always run 'lark-cli schema wiki.<resource>.<method>' before making API calls to understand required parameters
Read the lark-shared/SKILL.md file first to understand authentication and permission requirements
Verify your CLI has the required permission scopes (e.g., wiki:node:read) before attempting operations
Use the specific node_id from your workspace when testing - examples may need adaptation
anti_patterns: Do not guess parameter formats for API calls - always check schema first as warned in the documentation
Do not skip reading the shared authentication setup as each wiki method requires specific scopes
Do not use this skill without having lark-cli binary installed on your system
Do not attempt to modify node structure using only the get_node method - additional methods may be needed

faq.question: What is the lark-wiki skill used for?
faq.answer: The lark-wiki skill helps you manage Lark (Feishu) knowledge bases through CLI commands. You can query wiki space nodes, view API schemas, and retrieve document node information.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you must have the lark-cli binary installed on your system. The skill also references a shared authentication file that should be read before making any API calls.

faq.question: Why should I check the schema before calling APIs?
faq.answer: The schema command shows you the exact parameter structure and data formats required by each API method. This prevents errors from incorrectly formatted requests and saves debugging time.

faq.question: What permissions do I need to use wiki operations?
faq.answer: Each wiki method requires specific permission scopes. For example, spaces.get_node requires the wiki:node:read scope. Check the permission table in the skill documentation.

faq.question: Can I create or modify wiki nodes with this skill?
faq.answer: The current implementation primarily documents the spaces.get_node method for retrieving node information. Additional methods for creating or modifying nodes may be available in the lark-cli tool.

faq.question: What should I do if I get permission errors?
faq.answer: First, read the lark-shared/SKILL.md file to verify authentication setup. Then check that your API token has the required scopes listed in the permission table for the method you are calling.
